messages.text: You don't have to be great to start, but you have to start to be great.
messages.author: Zig Ziglar

messages.text: The computer was born to solve problems that did not exist before.
messages.author: Bill Gates

messages.text: It works on my machine.
messages.author: Every developer, ever

messages.text: A ship in harbor is safe, but that is not what ships are for.
messages.author: John A. Shedd

messages.text: First, solve the problem. Then, write the code.
messages.author: John Johnson

messages.text: The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second best time is now.
messages.author: Chinese Proverb

messages.text: There are only two hard things in computer science: cache invalidation, naming things, and off-by-one errors.
messages.author: Phil Karlton (amended)

messages.text: Do what you can, with what you have, where you are.
messages.author: Theodore Roosevelt

messages.text: 99 little bugs in the code, 99 little bugs… Take one down, patch it around… 127 little bugs in the code.
messages.author: Anonymous

messages.text: You are enough. You have always been enough.
messages.author: Anonymous

messages.text: The only way to do great work is to love what you do.
messages.author: Steve Jobs

messages.text: Simplicity is the ultimate sophistication.
messages.author: Leonardo da Vinci

messages.text: Talk is cheap. Show me the code.
messages.author: Linus Torvalds

messages.text: Any fool can write code that a computer can understand. Good programmers write code that humans can understand.
messages.author: Martin Fowler

messages.text: In the middle of difficulty lies opportunity.
messages.author: Albert Einstein

messages.text: It's not a bug — it's an undocumented feature.
messages.author: Anonymous

messages.text: Be kind, for everyone you meet is fighting a hard battle.
messages.author: Ian Maclaren

messages.text: The quieter you become, the more you are able to hear.
messages.author: Ram Dass

messages.text: Debugging is twice as hard as writing the code in the first place.
messages.author: Brian W. Kernighan

messages.text: You are braver than you believe, stronger than you seem, and smarter than you think.
messages.author: A.A. Milne

messages.text: Done is better than perfect.
messages.author: Sheryl Sandberg

messages.text: sudo make me a sandwich.
messages.author: xkcd

messages.text: Fall seven times, stand up eight.
messages.author: Japanese Proverb

messages.text: The sun also rises.
messages.author: Ernest Hemingway

messages.text: It always seems impossible until it's done.
messages.author: Nelson Mandela

messages.text: A day without laughter is a day wasted.
messages.author: Charlie Chaplin

messages.text: Real programmers count from 0.
messages.author: Anonymous

messages.text: You can't go back and change the beginning, but you can start where you are and change the ending.
messages.author: C.S. Lewis

messages.text: Perfection is achieved not when there is nothing more to add, but when there is nothing left to take away.
messages.author: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry

messages.text: Have you tried turning it off and on again?
messages.author: The IT Crowd

messages.text: Small acts of kindness change the world.
messages.author: Anonymous

messages.text: Keep going. You're doing better than you think.
messages.author: Anonymous

messages.text: The best error message is the one that never shows up.
messages.author: Thomas Fuchs

messages.text: One must imagine Sisyphus happy.
messages.author: Albert Camus

messages.text: Git blame says it was you. Git blame is always right.
messages.author: Anonymous

messages.text: The world is full of people who have stopped listening to themselves.
messages.author: Joseph Campbell

messages.text: Act as if what you do makes a difference. It does.
messages.author: William James

messages.text: Weekends don't count unless you spend them doing something completely pointless.
messages.author: Bill Watterson

messages.text: The most important thing is to try and inspire people so that they can be great in whatever they want to do.
messages.author: Kobe Bryant

messages.text: Breathe. It's just a bad day, not a bad life.
messages.author: Anonymous
